title: Vitana Voice Pipeline Canonical Spec
description: Single source of truth for the surface that BOTH the Vertex (TypeScript, services/gateway/src/routes/orb-live.ts) and LiveKit (Python, services/agents/orb-agent/) implementations must expose. The voice-pipeline-parity-scanner CI job runs ts-morph and libcst extractors on each side and three-way diffs against this file. Drift here is the merge gate, not vibes.

tools.name: recall_conversation_at_time
tools.notes: Time-anchored conversation recall.

tools.name: search_memory
tools.notes: Mem0/Qdrant semantic memory search via memory-indexer service.

tools.name: search_knowledge
tools.notes: Knowledge Hub retrieval via gateway retrieval-router.

tools.name: switch_persona
tools.notes: Within-Vitana style/persona switch (NOT specialist handoff). Specialist handoff is `report_to_specialist`.

tools.name: report_to_specialist
tools.notes: Unified Feedback Pipeline persona handoff (Sage / Devon / Atlas / Mira / Vitana).

tools.name: consult_external_ai
tools.notes: Forward to user's connected ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini account.

tools.name: get_life_compass
tools.notes: Returns the user's active Life Compass row (goal + why + target_date). Read-only. Shared dispatcher route.

tools.name: save_diary_entry
tools.notes: Persists diary entry AND triggers Vitana Index recompute via /memory/diary/sync-index.

tools.name: resolve_recipient
tools.notes: Step 1 of 3-step send_message flow.

tools.name: send_chat_message
tools.notes: Step 3 of send_message flow.

tools.name: get_autopilot_recommendations
tools.notes: Read the user's Autopilot queue aloud — same list as the popup. Session-state-coupled (remembers listed ids).

tools.name: activate_autopilot_recommendations
tools.notes: Activate Autopilot recommendations on the user's behalf via the shared popup flow. Resolves 'activate those' to the last listed ids.

tools.name: find_match
tools.notes: BOOTSTRAP-FIND-MATCH-VOICE: search-first 'find me a match' — searches the catalog and recommends matches (also posting so the user is discoverable) or posts on confirmation. In the shared ORB registry; LiveKit can dispatch it but does not declare it yet (mirrors view_intent_matches).

tools.name: find_perfect_product
tools.notes: Recommends a product (supplement/gear/food) fusing weakest pillar + Life Compass goal. Shared dispatcher tool_find_perfect_product. Surfaced to LiveKit in VTID-03048 (previously Vertex-only catalog despite shared impl).

tools.name: find_perfect_practitioner
tools.notes: Recommends a practitioner/coach/doctor (specialty + language + telehealth + price). Shared dispatcher tool_find_perfect_practitioner. Surfaced to LiveKit in VTID-03048 (previously Vertex-only catalog despite shared impl).

tools.name: navigate
tools.notes: Unified surface-aware navigation tool exposed to the model (VTID-NAV-UNIFIED — replaces the old navigator_consult + navigate_to_screen dance). Cross-surface routes (vitanaland→/admin or /command-hub) MUST be rejected per feedback_navigator_surface_scoping memory rule. Vertex dispatches via toolName if-chain (not a switch), so the ts-morph extractor cannot see it; LiveKit declares it as @function_tool navigate.

tools.name: get_current_screen
tools.notes: Identity-free read of session.current_route — safe for anonymous sessions. Vertex dispatches via toolName if-chain (not extracted by ts-morph); LiveKit declares it as @function_tool get_current_screen.

tools.name: navigate_to_screen
tools.notes: INTENTIONALLY ONE-SIDED (Vertex-only): catalog-aware navigation taking a screen_id/alias, preserving parameterized routes (intent_id, match_id) and overlay support that the free-text `navigate` tool does not. LiveKit exposes only `navigate`. Cross-surface routes MUST be rejected per feedback_navigator_surface_scoping memory rule.

tools.name: navigator_consult
tools.notes: INTENTIONALLY ONE-SIDED (Vertex-only): legacy consult-then-narrate path that predates the unified `navigate` tool. Still routed to handleNavigate for in-flight sessions that cached the old tool declarations. No LiveKit equivalent. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: find_community_member
tools.notes: Shared dispatcher tool — implemented on both sides. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY parity-scan audit (was undeclared).

tools.name: log_water
tools.notes: Diary quick-log: water. Shared dispatcher tool, both sides. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: log_sleep
tools.notes: Diary quick-log: sleep. Shared dispatcher tool, both sides. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: log_exercise
tools.notes: Diary quick-log: exercise. Shared dispatcher tool, both sides. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: log_meditation
tools.notes: Diary quick-log: meditation. Shared dispatcher tool, both sides. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: get_pillar_subscores
tools.notes: Returns per-pillar Vitana Index subscores. Shared dispatcher tool, both sides. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: teacher_event
tools.notes: INTENTIONALLY ONE-SIDED (Vertex-only): teaching-session telemetry event. No LiveKit @function_tool equivalent has shipped. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: end_teaching_session
tools.notes: INTENTIONALLY ONE-SIDED (Vertex-only): closes an active teaching session. No LiveKit @function_tool equivalent has shipped. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

tools.name: get_social_context
tools.notes: BOOTSTRAP-SOCIAL-MEMORY: live privacy-filtered Social Context Pack (follows, matches, messages, groups, person intelligence, ranked posts/events). In the shared registry (reachable via /api/v1/orb/tool); LiveKit @function_tool wrapper not yet shipped — Vertex-only until it is.

tools.name: view_messages
tools.notes: BOOTSTRAP-SOCIAL-READ-TOOLS: speakable read of the user's own INTERNAL community inbox (unread/all, grouped by sender). Never Google-bound; 'archived' does not exist. In the shared registry; LiveKit @function_tool wrapper not yet shipped — Vertex-only until it is.

tools.name: list_followers
tools.notes: BOOTSTRAP-SOCIAL-READ-TOOLS: who follows the user (count, names, mutuals) — direct answer, never deflects. In the shared registry; LiveKit @function_tool wrapper not yet shipped — Vertex-only until it is.

tools.name: list_following
tools.notes: BOOTSTRAP-SOCIAL-READ-TOOLS: who the user follows (count and names). In the shared registry; LiveKit @function_tool wrapper not yet shipped — Vertex-only until it is.

tools.name: recent_conversations
tools.notes: BOOTSTRAP-SOCIAL-READ-TOOLS: newest-first DM contacts; first entry answers 'who did I last chat with'. In the shared registry; LiveKit @function_tool wrapper not yet shipped — Vertex-only until it is.

tools.name: get_newest_member

tools.name: get_most_followed
tools.notes: Get the community member with the most followers.

tools.name: ask_who_is
tools.notes: Answer ANY free-form 'who is...?' superlative question about the

tools.name: check_calendar_conflicts

tools.name: complete_reminder

tools.name: list_missed_reminders

tools.name: set_alarm
tools.notes: Set a wake-up/clock alarm at a specific time of day (optionally recurring).

tools.name: list_alarms
tools.notes: List the user's active alarms with times and labels.

tools.name: delete_alarm
tools.notes: Cancel an alarm. Two-step confirm flow: first call WITHOUT confirm to find

tools.name: start_timer
tools.notes: Start a countdown timer (1-1440 minutes).

tools.name: start_pomodoro
tools.notes: Start a pomodoro focus block (5-90 minutes; 25 if omitted).

tools.name: list_active_timers
tools.notes: List running timers and pomodoros with the remaining time on each.

tools.name: get_world_time
tools.notes: Get the current local time in a city or IANA timezone (no internet needed).

tools.name: list_my_groups
tools.notes: List the community groups the user belongs to, with member counts.

tools.name: create_group
tools.notes: Create a new community group. Requires a name; privacy is public or

tools.name: join_group
tools.notes: Join a community group by name or group_id. Resolves fuzzy names and

tools.name: invite_to_group

tools.name: decline_invitation

tools.name: rsvp_event
tools.notes: RSVP / sign the user up for a community event or meetup, by event_id

tools.name: cancel_rsvp
tools.notes: Cancel the user's RSVP for an upcoming event. ALWAYS call once

tools.name: list_upcoming_meetups
tools.notes: List upcoming community meetups/events the user could attend, soonest

tools.notes: Join/open a community live room by name or room_id. Returns a

tools.notes: Mark direct messages as read. With member set, marks that conversation;

tools.notes: Set the visibility of the user's WHOLE profile: public, followers_only, or private.

tools.notes: File a bug ticket (kind=bug) in the feedback pipeline. No persona swap —

tools.notes: List the user's OPEN feedback tickets (bugs, support, disputes, account)

tools.name: set_language
tools.notes: Set the user's app + voice language. Persists the same setting the app's

tools.notes: Take down one of the user's OWN intent posts from the board (closes it;

tools.notes: Open a dispute on a match the user is part of (no-show, misrepresented,

tools.notes: Flagship people-match: find the PERFECT person for the user — workout

tools.notes: Read the user's current emotional and cognitive signals (D28): mood,

tools.notes: Check how available and ready the user is right now (D33): availability

tools.notes: Read the user's environment and mobility context (D34): where they

tools.notes: Read the user's life-stage context (D40): life phase and stability,

tools.name: follow_member
tools.notes: FOLLOW a community member by their spoken name.

tools.name: unfollow_member
tools.notes: UNFOLLOW a community member by name. Two-step confirm:

tools.name: get_notifications
tools.notes: READ the user's recent notifications, unread first. Speakable.

tools.name: mark_notifications_read
tools.notes: MARK notifications as read — all unread ones, or only those whose title

tools.notes: LIKE a community feed post — resolves 'the last post from <name>' to that

tools.notes: COMMENT on a community feed post (public text). Two-step confirm:

tools.name: dev_list_vtids
tools.notes: DEVELOPER ONLY. List recent VTID tasks from the ledger (id, title, status).

tools.name: dev_get_vtid_status
tools.notes: DEVELOPER ONLY. Get one VTID task by id — status, spec status, terminal state, claim.

tools.name: dev_list_pending_approvals
tools.notes: DEVELOPER ONLY. List PRs/actions waiting in the approvals queue (same queue as the Command Hub approvals view).

tools.name: dev_count_approvals
tools.notes: DEVELOPER ONLY. Count how many items are pending approval.

tools.notes: DEVELOPER ONLY. Approve a queued approval — merges its PR via the governed pipeline.

tools.name: dev_get_routine_detail

tools.name: dev_list_active_healing

tools.name: dev_get_autonomy_pulse
tools.notes: DEVELOPER ONLY. One-shot autonomy status: pending findings, pending heals, executions

tools.name: dev_list_agents
tools.notes: DEVELOPER ONLY. List registered agents with heartbeat-derived health (healthy/degraded/down).

oasis_topics.notes: INTENTIONALLY ONE-SIDED (LiveKit-only): per-tool call/result telemetry namespace. Vertex emits the unified orb.live.tool.executed instead. Declared in BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY audit.

watchdogs.name: ip_geo_cache_ttl_ms
watchdogs.description: 6 h — IP_GEO_CACHE_TTL_MS in orb-live.ts (`// 6 hours`) is canonical; spec previously claimed 1 h (BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY value_mismatch fix). LiveKit's watchdogs.py uses 1 h but does not gate this constant via the spec (implementations: vertex only).

watchdogs.name: max_reconnects
watchdogs.description: Max reconnects per session (~50 min total). VTID-STREAM-RECONNECT.

watchdogs.name: greeting_prebuffer_fallback_ms
watchdogs.description: DEV-COMHU-0513 — GREETING_PREBUFFER_FALLBACK_MS in orb-live.ts. When FEATURE_ORB_GREETING_PREBUFFER_ENV is live, the WS greeting prompt is dispatched on connect and its audio held until the client's audio_ready; if that ack never arrives, the held greeting is flushed after this timeout so the user is never left in silence.

watchdogs.name: token_refresh_skew_ms
watchdogs.description: 5 min — refresh the cached ADC access token this far ahead of expiry so getAccessToken() never blocks the live-connect path

watchdogs.name: token_default_ttl_ms
watchdogs.description: 55 min — fallback ADC token TTL when the auth client doesn't expose expiry_date

watchdogs.name: tool_cache_ttl_ms
watchdogs.description: 2 min — per-session cache TTL for read-only retrieval tools (search_memory/search_knowledge); repeat lookups skip the blocking function_response round-trip (BOOTSTRAP-ORB-LATENCY-PHASE2)

watchdogs.name: vertex_bootstrap_cache_ttl_ms
watchdogs.description: 5 min — per-identity cache TTL for buildBootstrapContextPack on the Vertex session-start path; filled by /live/session/prewarm so the first tap skips the 400-800ms context build (BOOTSTRAP-ORB-LATENCY-PHASE2)

documented_invariants.description: Behaviours required by both implementations but enforced by inline checks rather than named constants. The parity scanner does not statically verify these (yet) — they live in spec.json so reviewers know the invariant exists, and to anchor future PRs that promote them to named constants.

items.name: vad_silence_ms_range
items.description: VAD silence-duration tunable bounds (VTID-RESPONSE-DELAY). Inline range check in /live/session/start payload validation.

items.name: tool_loop_guard_max_consecutive
items.description: Force-flush after 5 consecutive tool calls without user-visible audio output (VTID-TOOLGUARD). On LiveKit, equivalent is AgentSession(max_tool_steps=5).

impl_specific_optional.description: Trailing OPTIONAL parameters that exist on only one implementation and are intentionally NOT part of the shared contract. The scanner strips these (after case-normalisation) before comparing signatures, so they do not register as drift. Adding a new name here is an explicit declaration that a parameter is one-sided on purpose.

system_instruction_params.notes: These are the parameter NAMES, not the prompt template. Each implementation owns its own template; only the shared parameter list must match across implementations. Cross-language case convention is NOT drift: the scanner normalises every name to `case_convention` (snake_case) before comparing, so TS `voiceStyle` and Python `voice_style` are considered equal. Names listed in `impl_specific_optional` are stripped before comparison (intentional one-sided params). Drift in any remaining shared name is a parity violation; drift in the template is not (the system_instruction golden-file test catches template drift separately, on the same fixed input).

next_steps: ship voice-pipeline-spec/ folder + extractor + CI in this PR
extract real arg JSON Schemas for each tool from orb-live.ts (this v0.1 has names + flags but not arg schemas)
wire the LiveKit-side codegen target once services/agents/orb-agent/ ships
promote the CI from report-only to merge gate once 30 days of green runs are recorded

EXTRACTOR GAP (BOOTSTRAP-VOICE-TOOL-PARITY): extract-ts.ts only walks orb-live.ts. The 11 remaining `missing_in_vertex` items are false negatives — they ARE implemented on the Vertex side, just outside that single file: (a) OASIS topics orb.live.tool.executed (orb/live/session/upstream-message-handler.ts), orb.navigator.requested/dispatched/blocked (services/orb-tools-shared.ts, upstream-message-handler.ts), feedback.ticket.created (services/report-to-specialist-core.ts); (b) watchdogs session_timeout_ms, conversation_timeout_ms, max_connections_per_ip, max_reconnects, max_history_chars, extraction_throttle_ms — all imported from ../orb/live/config. Fix is to widen the extractor's file set to the orb/live module tree (separate PR — out of scope for the spec-reconciliation pass). These stay declared as vertex because they are genuinely implemented.
